seoTitle: Find a Phone Number in Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Locate your own direct dial number, a colleague's contact number, or a meeting's dial-in bridge inside Microsoft Teams.
intro: Whether you need to check your own assigned work number, find a teammate's contact details, or grab dial-in information for a meeting, Teams organizes phone data across a few different panels. This guide covers all four common lookup scenarios.

steps.heading: Check your own number
steps.body: Click the Calls icon in the left sidebar and look above the dial pad, where your personal direct dial number is displayed if one is assigned.

steps.heading: Find a colleague's number
steps.body: Open a chat with them and click their name avatar to trigger their profile card, then select contact information to see their listed phone number.

steps.heading: Search the company directory
steps.body: Open the integrated People app through the three-dot more options menu and search the company-wide database for a specific team member.

steps.heading: Get a meeting's dial-in bridge
steps.body: Open a scheduled meeting invite in the Calendar hub to view its automated PSTN dial-in number and conference ID.

steps.heading: Audit numbers as an administrator
steps.body: System administrators can log into the Teams Admin Center and navigate to Voice, then Phone numbers, to review the organization's full allocation.

faq.question: Why don't I see a phone number above my dial pad?
faq.answer: Not every user has an assigned PSTN direct-dial number — this depends on your organization's calling plan setup.

faq.question: Can I find any coworker's phone number through Teams?
faq.answer: Yes, if their number is populated in the directory, their profile card or the People app will display it.

faq.question: How do I get the dial-in number for a specific meeting?
faq.answer: Open the meeting's calendar invite, which lists the country-specific dial-in number along with the conference ID.

faq.question: Who can view the full organizational phone number inventory?
faq.answer: That level of access is reserved for system administrators using the Voice section of the Teams Admin Center.
